sourceMovie.title: Papillon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Papillon (2017) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Shawshank Redemption
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Papillon clicked for you, The Shawshank Redemption (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Papillon?
faq.answer: Try The Shawshank Redemption, The Green Mile, Extremely Wicked, Shockingly Evil and Vile—each shares DNA with Papillon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Papillon?
faq.answer: Papillon (2017) is best described as Adventure, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Papillon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
